info.name: MedBridge-A — Auth API (v2)
info.description:
Auth endpoints for MedBridge-A (register, login, refresh, /me, logout). Verified live 2026-07-01 against the /api/v1/auth prefix. Tokens chain via collection variables: login captures the access token into {{access_token}}; the refresh token rides as an httpOnly cookie (Path=/api/v1/auth) that Postman sends automatically — no body-token workaround needed. Set {{base_url}} to your running server.

RUN ORDER: 1 Register → 2 Login → 3 Refresh → 4 /me → 5 Logout → 6 Refresh-after-logout (negative). Run top to bottom so the token chain works.

NOTE on audit logging: the backend now writes an audit_log row for each auth event (login.success, login.failure, logout, token.refresh, token.reuse_detected). Postman can't assert DB rows directly — verify those in psql:
  SELECT action, user_id, created_at FROM audit_log ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 10;

request.description: Creates a user. Success: 200 with {id, email, is_active} — no password/hash in the response. Errors: 400 duplicate email, 422 malformed/missing field. NOTE: register returns 200 today; a 200→201 change is under discussion — if it lands, update the assertion below to 201.

exec: // Register may 200 (created) or 400 (already exists) — both are acceptable on a re-run.
pm.test('never returns a password or hash', function () {

request.description:
Authenticates. Success: 200 with {access_token} ONLY (no token_type, no user field — see note) plus a Set-Cookie refresh_token (httpOnly, Path=/api/v1/auth, SameSite=lax). Errors: 401 for wrong password OR unknown email (same message — deliberately doesn't leak which). Saves the access token to {{access_token}}; the refresh cookie is stored by Postman's cookie jar and sent automatically to /api/v1/auth/* requests below.

OPEN CONTRACT ITEM: the frontend expects a `user` object on login; the backend currently returns only access_token. Pending decision: add user (+role) to this response, or have the FE fetch /api/v1/auth/me after login.

exec: // NOTE: login currently returns ONLY {access_token} — no token_type, no user.
// If the team adds those to the login response, extend this test accordingly.
pm.test('refresh token is a cookie, not in the body', function () {
pm.test('refresh cookie is scoped to /api/v1/auth', function () {

request.description: Exchanges the refresh cookie for a NEW access token and rotates the refresh token. No body — the httpOnly cookie (Path=/api/v1/auth) is sent automatically by Postman. Success: 200 with a new access_token + a new Set-Cookie. Reuse of an already-rotated token → 401 and ALL of that user's sessions are revoked (reuse-detection security feature).

exec: pm.test('returns a new access token', function () {
// Store the rotated access token for /me below.

request.description: Returns the current user. Requires Authorization: Bearer <access token>. Success: 200 with {id, email, is_active}. Errors: 401 missing/invalid/expired token. Uses {{access_token}} captured at login/refresh. NOTE: this endpoint lives on the AUTH router — full path is /api/v1/auth/me (not /users/me).

exec: pm.test('never leaks a password or hash', function () {

request.description: Revokes the refresh token and clears the cookie. Cookie-only — the httpOnly refresh cookie (Path=/api/v1/auth) is sent automatically; no body needed. (This replaces the old body-token workaround: the cookie-path bug that required it is fixed — cookie is now scoped /api/v1/auth so it reaches /logout.) Success: 200 {detail:'Logged out'}. After this, Refresh (request 6) should 401. Missing cookie → 401 'Missing refresh token'.

Negative test: after logout the refresh token is revoked, so this should 401. Confirms logout actually killed the session (and that the cookie-path fix works — if the cookie weren't reaching /logout, the session wouldn't have been revoked and this would wrongly 200).

exec: pm.test('status is 401 (session was revoked by logout)', function () {
